title: Gherkin/BDD prompt language experiment for user authentication REST API

generation_target.summary: Generate a user authentication REST API with registration, login, JWT token management, protected resource access, account lockout, and password hashing — all behaviors defined by Gherkin scenarios.
deliverables: Registration endpoint with email/password validation and duplicate rejection.
Login endpoint with JWT access tokens, refresh tokens, and enumeration resistance.
Token refresh endpoint that rejects expired refresh tokens.
Protected resource endpoint requiring valid access token.
Account lockout after 5 consecutive failed login attempts.
Password hashing with bcrypt — never store or return plaintext.
Focused tests covering every scenario from the feature file.
non_goals: Do not build OAuth2 or social login.
Do not implement email verification or password reset.
Do not add role-based access control or admin functionality.
Do not use a production database — in-memory storage only.
Do not add logging, monitoring, or deployment configuration.

prompt_variants.label: Hybrid Gherkin plus English guidance prompt
Gherkin plus explicit acceptance criteria prompt

fairness_rules: All variants target the same user authentication API generation task.
The hybrid prompt may add implementation guidance but must not expand the required scenarios.
The acceptance criteria variant may add verification requirements but must not widen scope beyond the same scenarios.
Prompt reviewers should compare deliverables and non-goals before any full-matrix run.
ownership_notes: This repo owns the experiment assets, manifest, and Gherkin evaluation utilities.
Authoritative harness and packaged report changes still belong in amplihack-agent-eval.
